item.title: Client-side full-text search
item.intent: The site must have Pagefind-powered static search across all content tracks — series, commentary, architecture KB, and products — with a keyboard-triggered overlay (Cmd/Ctrl+K). Build-time index, zero runtime JS cost until activated.
item.description: Install Pagefind, run it as a post-build step to index dist/, create a search overlay component triggered by Cmd/Ctrl+K. Pagefind handles indexing and UI — style the overlay to match the dark theme with amber accents. Add a search icon/trigger to the Nav component.
steps: Install pagefind as a dev dependency: npm install --save-dev pagefind
Add a post-build script in package.json that runs pagefind --site dist/ after astro build (update the build script to: astro check && astro build && pagefind --site dist/)

Create src/components/SearchOverlay.astro with: (a) a fixed overlay div with id="search-overlay", (b) a vanilla JS inline script with Cmd/Ctrl+K keyboard listener using e.metaKey||e.ctrlKey, (c) lazy dynamic import of /pagefind/pagefind-ui.js on first activation, (d) dark theme CSS overrides with amber accents matching --color-primary
Import SearchOverlay in src/layouts/Base.astro and render it inside <body> before </body> — this step is required for the overlay HTML to appear in all built pages
Add a search trigger button to src/components/Nav.astro that calls window.__openSearch() on click, with a search icon and keyboard shortcut hint (⌘K)
Verify the build produces dist/pagefind/ and that dist/index.html contains the search-overlay element

acceptanceCriteria.text: Pagefind index is generated at build time
Search overlay element is present in built pages
Pagefind script is referenced in built pages
